behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit looked possible for HMAC comparison alone, but the failure boundary includes handler→queue handoff ("body is not forwarded"). Pass 2: keep Integration because this is cross-module gating (HTTP handler + verifier + queue interface). Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; in-process request + queue spy can expose forwarding bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler in-process with a real signature-verification module and a spy/fake queue adapter. Send a request with a tampered body/signature pair; assert HTTP 401 and assert zero enqueue calls.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires module interaction (reject + no-forward), not just pure hash logic.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration because ordering is between handler and persistence layer. Pass 2: keep Integration; to catch real bug, use actual persistence-backed queue module (local DB/filesystem) and assert write completes before response. Rejected System: behavior text is about sequencing, not real external network/OS effects beyond local module wiring. Rejected higher AI levels: none involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to the real persistent queue implementation (local test storage). Instrument queue write completion and response send time; assert durable write timestamp precedes HTTP 200 completion.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses persistence + handler ordering; lower-level unit mock could miss durability/await issues.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration since this is decoupling between request path and downstream processor module. Pass 2: keep Integration; a controllable long-running downstream stub reveals blocking regressions. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real subprocess/network, only async boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start handler with downstream processor mocked to block (e.g., unresolved promise) after enqueue. Send valid webhook and assert HTTP response completes promptly while processor remains unfinished.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Directly validates module decoupling; no real OS/network artifact required.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System due to wall-clock SLO under concurrency. Pass 2: keep System; this failure can depend on real event-loop scheduling, socket backlog, and HTTP server behavior under concurrent load, which in-process fakes often hide. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" and "slow and concurrent" conditions that need real runtime characteristics. Rejected AI levels: no LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process, generate concurrent requests where earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency for new valid requests; assert p(ack<3s) meets requirement.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real timing/concurrency behavior; lower-level tests can miss scheduler/backpressure effects.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration because dedupe correctness sits at handler + idempotency store/queue boundary. Pass 2: keep Integration; verify with real dedupe path (e.g., unique key in local DB-backed queue) to catch handoff/state bugs. Rejected System: behavior text says duplicate ID logic, not OS/network-specific failure mode. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same event ID twice through the handler against the real local persistence/dedupe layer; assert only one queue record exists and second request is handled per API contract without a second write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration because it validates translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP contract. Pass 2: keep Integration; a queue module that returns full/error is sufficient to expose mapping bugs. Rejected System: no required real OS/network artifact in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue implementation (or boundary fake) that deterministically returns capacity-full on enqueue; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Tests module interaction and error mapping; no external infra required.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration because this is queue + persistence/storage behavior, not a single pure function. Pass 2: keep Integration; must run real queue implementation against local storage to catch off-by-one/transaction/state bugs that mocks hide. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real external network or OS primitives beyond local storage. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity in test storage, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one extra; assert explicit rejection and no storage growth beyond configured cap.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real queue/persistence wiring; lower unit test with stubs can miss true capacity enforcement.
